game_name: Commander Keen: Secret of the Oracle

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures with distinct animated sprites moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring evasion or combat avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, pits, and falling platforms rendered with contrasting colors
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats that cause instant damage or death

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more ornate enemy sprites with exaggerated animations in contained arenas

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing or highlighted objects (gems, power-ups) placed on safe platforms
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to approach; provides resource or temporary benefit

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable platform tiles with consistent coloring and defined edges
Distinctive architectural elements or glowing markers
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone where progress is preserved

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinctive protagonist sprite with recognizable outfit and pogo stick
Oracle and mystical architectural elements as environmental motif
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ancient temple and mystical ruins aesthetic throughout levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Simpler enemy types, clearer safe pathways, brighter color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol in predictable patterns; fewer simultaneous threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasingly complex level layouts with layered platforms; more detailed backgrounds revealing story elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy behavior becomes more aggressive; environmental hazards increase in frequency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual isolation of arena; dramatic lighting or color shift; larger enemy sprite centered on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Music intensifies; boss performs telegraphed attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temporary visual feedback on player sprite or screen border glow
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased mobility, invulnerability, or enhanced attack capability

environmental_storytelling.example: Level design gradually transitions from natural caves to constructed Gnosticae temples, indicating approach to civilization center
Increasing density and sophistication of enemy types suggests deeper penetration into dangerous territory
Parallax scrolling backgrounds and decorative elements reveal mythological/sci-fi context of ancient oracle civilization
Fewer collectibles in specific areas creates tension; abundance signals safe zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves in fixed pattern, ignores player at distance; sprite animation shows routine motion
Animation speed increases; movement becomes erratic or directly pursues player
Sprite disappears or plays brief death animation; often rewards points or item drop
Brief invulnerability flash (sprite flickers); health counter decreases visually
Sprite gains visual enhancement (glow, size increase, or outfit change); movement speed or jump height increases

notes: Commander Keen: Secret of the Oracle uses classic platformer visual language. Danger is communicated through enemy sprite design (hostile appearance), environmental hazard placement (visual distinctiveness), and level architecture (increasing complexity). Safety signals rely on collectible item design and platform stability. World state progresses through level-based chapters with visual escalation toward the oracle. Faction identity is minimal—world is primarily player vs. environment/creatures, with the Gnosticae civilization existing as environmental context rather than active political force. Visual communication is entirely graphical with no dialogue or text-based world-state indicators.
sources_used: tcrf.net (attempted source contact returned restricted content notice; extraction based on documented game mechanics for Commander Keen series T2-tier knowledge)
